evidence.text: Patient is a 65-year-old male with a history of hypertension and diabetes. He presents with a 2-day history of fever, chills, and cough. Vital signs: temperature 38.5 C, blood pressure 90/60 mmHg, heart rate 110 bpm. Physical exam reveals lung crackles and wheezing.
He presents with a 2-day history of fever, chills, and cough.

Patient was admitted with suspected sepsis and was started on broad-spectrum antibiotics. Patient's condition improved with treatment, with lactate levels decreasing to 1.5 mmol/L and creatinine decreasing to 1.0 mg/dL. Patient's platelet count increased to 100 10^9/L, and blood pressure improved to 100/60 mmHg. Patient was weaned off vasopressors and was able to be extubated.

Physical exam reveals lung crackles and wheezing. Lab results: lactate 2.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, platelets 80 10^9/L. Patient is suspected to have sepsis and will be started on broad-spectrum antibiotics. Will monitor vital signs and lab results closely.

Patient is requiring increasing amounts of oxygen and is being considered for intubation. Vasopressors have been started to support blood pressure. Patient's altered mentation is a concern, with a Glasgow Coma Scale score of 12. Will continue to monitor patient's condition closely and adjust treatment as needed.

Will monitor vital signs and lab results closely. Patient's MAP is 60 mmHg, which is concerning for hypotension. Will consider starting vasopressors if blood pressure does not improve. Nursing: patient tolerating oral intake as ordered.
Will consider starting vasopressors if blood pressure does not improve.
Vasopressors have been started to support blood pressure.

Patient was weaned off vasopressors and was able to be extubated. Patient's altered mentation improved, with a Glasgow Coma Scale score of 15. Patient was discharged home with follow-up appointments with primary care and infectious disease specialists. Nursing: patient tolerating oral intake as ordered.

Patient was weaned off vasopressors and was able to be extubated.

Patient is requiring increasing amounts of oxygen and is being considered for intubation. Vasopressors have been started to support blood pressure. Patient's altered mentation is a concern, with a Glasgow Coma Scale score of 12. Will continue to monitor patient's condition closely and adjust treatment as needed.
Patient's altered mentation is a concern, with a Glasgow Coma Scale score of 12.
Lab results: lactate 2.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, platelets 80 10^9/L.
Lactate level has increased to 3.0 mmol/L, creatinine has risen to 1.5 mg/dL, and platelets have decreased to 60 10^9/L.
Patient's condition improved with treatment, with lactate levels decreasing to 1.5 mmol/L and creatinine decreasing to 1.0 mg/dL.
Patient's MAP is 60 mmHg, which is concerning for hypotension.

## Coding rationale
Clinical validation of billed Sepsis should rest on documented infection plus organ dysfunction indicators (sepsis3_summary, Section: Organ dysfunction indicators (simplified for demo)). Findings that affect payment grouping must be supported by chart indicators (icd10cm_guidelines_fy2026_summary, Section: Sepsis coding notes (high level)). This draft is for auditor review only and is not a payment decision. Key evidence IDs considered: E1, E10, E11, E12, E13, E14, E15, E16, E17, E18, E19, E2, E20, E21, E22, E3, E4, E5, E6, E7, E8, E9.

## Reviewer note
Machine-drafted aid generated from synthetic data. A qualified auditor makes the final determination.
